name: Distance Timeline Overlay UI
updated: 2026-04-30 (axis: separate start/finish vs more points; marker label & size; per-band offsets)

intent.description: compact progress/timeline overlay for distance or activity progress
avoid: treating this as the bottom editor timeline
showing irrelevant controls for presets that do not use them

presets.label: Minimal
presets.description: compact translucent dark widget with value and slim progress bar

presets.label: Dense
presets.description: DaVinci-like technical panel with ticks and solid progress

presets.label: Sport
presets.description: sport-watch style with optional left media slot

presets.label: Splits
presets.description: kilometer ticks, current marker, start/finish context

presets.label: Glass
presets.description: named glass direction with fake glass background disabled until real blur/material support exists

presets.label: Lower Third
presets.description: broadcast lower-third with optional left media slot

presets.label: Route
presets.description: route/path-like progress with optional elevation profile

rules: tickDensity controls tick count independently from preset
dense and splits progress fills remain solid
primary value can be disabled so the component acts as a pure distance timeline
custom value rows appear only when customValuesEnabled is true
custom values are metric-driven, not manually typed text
customValuesGroupSpacing controls distance from primary value to the custom group
customValueSpacing controls distance between custom values
increasing customValuesGroupSpacing moves the whole custom group and must not compress custom values or reduce customValueSpacing
custom values render inline after the primary value on the same baseline
custom value style is independent from primary value style
valueProgressSpacing controls vertical distance between the value block and progress track
label is a separate section following Numeric overlay label behavior
labelValueSpacing controls vertical distance between label and value
showAxisLabels toggles start/finish pair only; showDistancePoints toggles intermediate distance labels only
axisEndpointPlacement and axisMidpointPlacement independently choose below or above the progress track
start/finish offset is stored as distancePointOffset for JSON compatibility; midpointAxisLabelOffset is separate
distance mode origin uses the same unit suffix as other distance labels
intermediate distance labels require More Points on and density greater than zero
marker color is independent from progress fill color
marker styles render as vector-native shapes in preview and export; SVG marker assets are not supported yet
marker distance label shows current activity distance with axis typography and requires Marker enabled
progress percentage is displayed through Stats Bar, not an inline Percent content toggle
inside is a separate boolean toggle from placement
inside supports top, bottom, left, and right placements
when inside is false offsets are distance from the whole component
when inside is true offsets are internal adjustment and must not cover the progress axis
left and right placements expand background height enough to cover all vertical slots
slot size is fixed per preset so text alignment does not jump
animated SVG controls are disabled until preview and export support exists
slot media must be project-local or project-packaged when persistence supports it

boundsRules: background and border include axis labels and distance point labels at the current distancePointGap
inside stats bars expand the main background and border using the current stats bar side, size, and offset
preview selection bounds use the same dynamic visual bounds as background and border
attached outside stats bar placements keep separate stats bar background
fadeRules: fade background and track layers first
primary value text remains full opacity by default
text fade is advanced and off by default

modelGaps: value unit system and custom value slots
axis label and distance point configuration

sharedStatsBarRenderer.baseline: Distance Timeline stats bar rendering behavior
rules: Distance Timeline and Route Map use the same stats bar rendering pipeline.
Stacked flow is used when placement is vertical or layout mode is stack.
itemSpacing is applied in both stacked and horizontal flows.
dividers render between slots in both stacked and horizontal flows.
